sentence: Subjective:
HPI: I've been experiencing frequent and severe headaches, which started when I was a teenager, initially related to my menstrual periods, but have worsened over time.
Recently, they've been triggered by stress and exercise.
The headaches are located on my right side, behind my eye, and feel like a pulsing sensation.
I've also experienced ringing in my ears for a few months and blurred vision on the side, which comes and goes.
I haven't experienced numbness or tingling in my hands, feet, or face.
I'm a runner and use Asics shoes for exercise.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: I've experienced headaches, ringing in my ears, and blurred vision.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: My heart exam showed a normal sinus rhythm with no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam revealed papilledema, or swelling behind the eye.
My cranial nerves one through twelve were intact, including a normal cranial reflex.
My gait was normal.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I am concerned that you may have idiopathic intracranial hypertension, a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; Suspected
Plan:
I will order an MRI to confirm the diagnosis and rule out other potential causes of your symptoms.
I will also perform a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and potentially relieve some of that pressure.
Additionally, I will refer you to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
My team will work with your insurance company to obtain preapproval for the MRI.
Follow-up: I will follow up with you once I have the results of the MRI and spinal tap to discuss the next steps in your treatment.
